name: sqlalchemy-stubs
documentation:
# Experimental SQLAlchemy type stubs and mypy plugin

This package contains type stubs and mypy plugin to provide more precise
static types and type inference for SQLAlchemy framework. SQLAlchemy uses
dynamic Python features that are hard to understand by static type checkers,
this is why the plugin is needed in addition to type stubs.

Currently, some basic use cases like inferring model field types are supported.
The final goal is to be able to get precise types for most common patterns.

## Installation

```
pip install sqlalchemy-stubs
```

Important: you need to enable the plugin in your mypy config file:
```
[mypy]
plugins = sqlmypy
```

functions.name: sqlmypy.fullname
documentation: Compatibility helper for mypy 0.750 vs older.
See https://github.com/python/mypy/issues/6617 for plugin API updates.

functions.name: sqlmypy.shortname
documentation: Compatibility helper for mypy 0.750 vs older.

functions.name: sqlmypy.is_declarative
documentation: Check if this is a subclass of a declarative base.

functions.name: sqlmypy.set_declarative
documentation: Record given class as a declarative base.

functions.name: sqlmypy.add_var_to_class
documentation:
Add a variable with given name and type to the symbol table of a class.

This also takes care about setting necessary attributes on the variable node.

functions.name: sqlmypy.add_model_init_hook
documentation:
Add a dummy __init__() to a model and record it is generated.

Instantiation will be checked more precisely when we inferred types
(using get_function_hook and model_hook).

functions.name: sqlmypy.add_metadata_var
documentation: Add .metadata attribute to a declarative base.

functions.name: sqlmypy.decl_deco_hook
sqlmypy.decl_info_hook
documentation:
Support dynamically defining declarative bases.

For example:
    from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

    Base = declarative_base()

functions.name: sqlmypy.model_hook
documentation:
More precise model instantiation check.

Note: sub-models are not supported.
Note: this is still not perfect, since the context for inference of
      argument types is 'Any'.

functions.name: sqlmypy.get_argument_by_name
documentation:
Return the expression for the specific argument.

This helper should only be used with non-star arguments.

functions.name: sqlmypy.get_argtype_by_name
documentation: Same as above but for argument type.

functions.name: sqlmypy.column_hook
documentation:
Infer better types for Column calls.

Examples:
    Column(String) -> Column[Optional[str]]
    Column(String, primary_key=True) -> Column[str]
    Column(String, nullable=False) -> Column[str]
    Column(String, default=...) -> Column[str]
    Column(String, default=..., nullable=True) -> Column[Optional[str]]

TODO: check the type of 'default'.

functions.name: sqlmypy.grouping_hook
documentation:
Infer better types for Grouping calls.

Examples:
    Grouping(text('asdf')) -> Grouping[None]
    Grouping(Column(String), nullable=False) -> Grouping[str]
    Grouping(Column(String)) -> Grouping[Optional[str]]

functions.name: sqlmypy.relationship_hook
documentation:
Support basic use cases for relationships.

Examples:
    from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship

    from one import OneModel
    if TYPE_CHECKING:
        from other import OtherModel

    class User(Base):
        __tablename__ = 'users'
        id = Column(Integer(), primary_key=True)
        one = relationship(OneModel)
        other = relationship("OtherModel")

This also tries to infer the type argument for 'RelationshipProperty'
using the 'uselist' flag.

functions.name: sqlmypy.parse_bool
documentation: We really need to add this to TypeChecker API

variables.name: sqlmypy.MYPY
documentation: we should support Python 3.5.1 and cases where typing_extensions is not available.

variables.name: sqlmypy.COLUMN_NAME
documentation: type: Final

variables.name: sqlmypy.CLAUSE_ELEMENT_NAME
documentation: type: Final

variables.name: sqlmypy.COLUMN_ELEMENT_NAME
documentation: type: Final

variables.name: sqlmypy.GROUPING_NAME
documentation: type: Final

variables.name: sqlmypy.RELATIONSHIP_NAME
documentation: type: Final

classes.name: sqlmypy.BasicSQLAlchemyPlugin
documentation:
Basic plugin to support simple operations with models.

Currently supported functionality:
  * Recognize dynamically defined declarative bases.
  * Add an __init__() method to models.
  * Provide better types for 'Column's and 'RelationshipProperty's
    using flags 'primary_key', 'nullable', 'uselist', etc.
